text:p.text:span: è har-ra-an kaskal si sá-sá-dè Ar

office:text.text:p: 10. sìg-sìg eden-na lib4-lib4 a-šà-ga ú-gu dé-e-neŠAr1:13

text:p.text:span: 15. x x ta hur-sag-gá x x x ba-ra

office:text.text:p: 18. [àga]-≠ús saÑ-Ñá 5 li-≠mu-um x x x [z]i–da á-gùb!-≠bu x[…]ArŠ1:21

text:p.text:span: saying (the words of) Aradmu, your servant:
to undertake an expedition to Subir,
to secure the taxes on the frontier territory,
to confer (with the elites of Subir) about Apilaåa, the high commissioner, and have them come to agreement,
so that he could bring to them (i.e. the Subir elites) up-to-date instructions
Alternative translation: so that as part of the daily instructions he could bring them (the people of Subir) good news.

office:text.text:p:
10 Drop (chasing) winds in the desert and robbers in the­ fields!3
Alternative translation: make winds and robbers disappear from the desert! 11 Until you have reached Apilaša, my high­ commissioner, 12 ignore all of this so that you can … your face before him (without delay),” ­13 so that their (local) dignitaries learn their orders, 14 so that your battle cries should cover the land(s). 15 … 16 staked poles/lances inlaid with silver, carnelian, and lapis … 17 nir-stone, gold, carnelian, lapis, … 18 Choice guards, five hundred strong, (stood to) his right and left …

text:p.text:span: This is an one-off attempt at creating a new letter from Aradmu to
ulgi by pasting together pieces of the first two letters of CKU, that is Ar
Ar1 (2). This student exercise ends inconclusively, as it becomes clear that the whole thing makes little sense. The text shares some unique variants with source X8 of Ar
1 (1) and both may come from the same place (e.g., the verbal form in line 9).
is simply wrong, resulting from lexical identification of both
may have been triggered by problems with understanding the use of the reduplicated predicate (see the commentary to
1: 5 above). The infelicitous alterations may have been motivated by a desire to create a completely new text based on the two source letters.
. This line, while it plays of the previous one, does not derive from any known letter. The only passage known to me that has the sequence
107, part of the “tetrad” used in early stages of education, but that is without the precious
) stone that opens the list here.
